trade.summary: Sweden exports machinery, vehicles (Volvo), telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), and pharmaceutical products to the UAE. The UAE exports crude oil and refined petroleum to Sweden. Swedish arms exports to UAE have been a domestic political controversy.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are functional but occasionally strained by Swedish domestic political criticism of UAE human rights practices. Sweden blocked some arms exports to UAE under pressure from civil society. Sweden's media freedom and press freedoms create some friction given UAE's media environment. Relations normalized through trade and investment channels.
recent_incidents: Swedish arms export restrictions to UAE debated in parliament

military.summary: Sweden (now a NATO member) has limited defense ties with the UAE. Swedish arms export policy has been restrictive toward Gulf states. Saab has sold some civilian aviation technology to UAE. No significant military cooperation.

financial.summary: UAE sovereign funds have some Swedish equity investments. Swedish pension funds (AP-fonden) have limited Gulf exposure. Ericsson and Volvo have significant UAE commercial operations. Swedish investment in UAE tech sector is growing.

energy.summary: Sweden imports limited volumes of UAE petroleum. Sweden's rapid renewable energy transition reduces dependence on Gulf hydrocarbons. Limited bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration in clean energy, sustainability, and ICT. Ericsson has major R&D partnerships with UAE telecom operators. Swedish universities cooperate with UAE institutions in sustainability research. KTH and Chalmers have UAE research linkages.
